label: I: Empty PQ-heavy (preloading)
description: Insert phase followed by point and empty-point reads.

label: II: Interleaved inserts, deletes, PQs, updates
description: Insert-heavy kvbench workload with deletes, empty reads, and updates.

label: III: Multi-distribution update and PQ (preloading)
description: Kvbench mix with updates, point queries, and empty-point queries.

label: IV: Update and range delete-heavy (preloading)
description: Kvbench workload combining updates with range deletes.

label: V: Insert-heavy with skewed prefixes
description: Single-group kvbench workload with inserts and point queries.

label: A: Update heavy
description: Balanced read and update workload with an initial insert phase.

label: B: Read heavy
description: Read-heavy workload with a small update slice.

label: C: Read only
description: Read-only YCSB workload after the initial insert phase.

label: D: Read latest
description: Read-latest YCSB workload with inserts and point queries.

label: E: Short ranges
description: Scan-heavy YCSB workload with inserts and range queries.

label: F: Read-Modify-Writes
description: Read-modify-write YCSB workload with point queries and merges.

label: 1: Multi-phased dynamic
description: Three phases: 1) Zipfian inserts/deletes, 2) Uniform empty point queries, 3) 50/50 insert/range queries.

label: 2: Interleaved multi-distributional
description: Complex mix of inserts, point/range queries, and deletes using Zipfian, Normal, Uniform, and Pareto distributions.

label: 3: Near-sorted ingestion
description: 100% insert workload designed to simulate nearly sorted data ingestion (K=1%, L=10%).

label: 4: Slow phase transitioning
description: Gradual shift from insert-heavy to read-heavy across three phases with varying range query selectivity.

label: 5: Discrete phase transitioning
description: Sharp transition from a write-heavy (90% insert) phase to a read-heavy (70% point query) phase.

label: 6: Variable entry size
description: 50/50 insert and point query mix where both keys and values vary significantly in size (32B–256B).

label: 7: Skewed access
description: Workload with high temporal locality; 90% of queries target a 10% 'hot' keys.

label: Test 1: Bulk Load (Random Order)
description: Loads 900M keys into an empty database in random order to measure raw ingestion performance.

label: Test 2: Random Read
description: Measures performance of random point lookups on the 900M key dataset.

label: Test 3: Multi-Random Read
description: Measures batched multi-get performance for existing keys.

label: Test 4: Range Scan (Forward)
description: Randomly selects starting points and iterates forward over keys to measure scan performance.

label: Test 4b: Reverse Range Scan
description: Randomly selects starting points and iterates backwards through the key space.

label: Test 5: Overwrite
description: Measures random overwrite performance on an existing database, triggering LSM-tree compactions.
